layout_description:
Option: OPT-1-REVISED
Slide description: TWO-COLUMN LAYOUT with 2:1 width allocation (left column dominant). The slide uses a clean, professional structure with the title 'Context and objectives' at the top, followed by two side-by-side content containers that fill the remaining vertical space confidently. The left container (Context) receives approximately 65% of the horizontal space to accommodate dense multi-paragraph content, while the right container (Objectives) receives approximately 30% for a compact numbered list. A 5% gutter separates the two columns. Both containers share identical top and bottom edges, creating strong horizontal alignment. The layout maximizes vertical space utilization — containers extend from just below the title to near the slide bottom, leaving only minimal safe-area margins. Content within both containers is distributed evenly across the full vertical height using flexGrow, eliminating any dead space at the bottom. Font sizes are increased from the previous attempt to ensure text confidently fills at least 65-70% of each container's height while maintaining within-slide uniformity (both columns use identical body text size). No decorative bottom layer is needed as the content containers fill the slide confidently. Reading flow is natural: title → left column (primary context) → right column (supporting objectives).

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.65, 0.3]

Containers:
  C0 (LEFT COLUMN — Context Section): This container holds the primary narrative content describing the Liquidity Solutions business context and ATLAS technology development. It occupies the left two-thirds of the slide body (split 2:1 with the right column). The container has no visible border or background fill — content sits directly on the white slide background for a clean, open feel. The section begins with a bold section header 'Context' in brand primary color, left-aligned at the top of the container. Below the header, content is organized as a vertical stack of bullet points using native PowerPoint bullets with brand-color circular bullet markers. The text uses body font, brand text color, left-aligned with standard bullet indentation. Content density is high (7 major bullets with nested sub-bullets describing the 2024/2025 stand-up, 2026 scaling plans, technology solution development, and today's meeting agenda), so padding is minimal — just enough to prevent text from crowding the container edges. CRITICAL FONT SIZE ADJUSTMENT: Body text font size must be increased to the medium-to-large range (targeting approximately 16-18pt, significantly larger than the previous 11-12pt) to ensure the text block fills at least 65-70% of the container's vertical height, eliminating the dead space that appeared at the bottom in the previous attempt. This font size must match exactly the font size used in C1 to preserve within-slide uniformity (rule C6). Bullet sections are distributed evenly across the full vertical height of the container using flexGrow on each major bullet section, ensuring no empty band accumulates at the bottom. Key phrases within bullets (dates like '2024/2025' and '2026', monetary values like '$250-300M' and '$1B+', technology names like 'ATLAS', 'Antares technology', 'NimbleGravity') are rendered in bold for emphasis. The container's left edge aligns to the slide's left safe-area margin; its right edge stops at approximately 65% of slide width to leave room for the objectives column. Top and bottom edges align exactly with the right column container (C1) to maintain horizontal row integrity across the two-column layout.
  C1 (RIGHT COLUMN — Objectives Section): This container holds a compact numbered list of three meeting objectives. It occupies the right third of the slide body (split 1:2 with the left column). The container has no visible border or background fill — content sits directly on the white slide background, matching the treatment of the left column. The section begins with a bold section header 'Objectives' in brand primary color, left-aligned at the top of the container. Below the header, content is organized as a numbered list (1, 2, 3) using native PowerPoint numbering with brand-color numbers. Each objective is a short text block: (1) 'Review outcomes from ATLAS Phase 1 and discussion what will be delivered in Phase 2 [10 mins]', (2) 'Demo ATLAS Phase 1 solution e.g., workflow tool including Borrower one-pagers [10 mins]', (3) 'Recall the value creation potential of Liquidity Solutions business and discuss funding requirements for ATLAS Phase 2 [10 mins]'. Text uses body font, brand text color, left-aligned with standard list indentation. CRITICAL FONT SIZE ADJUSTMENT: Body text font size must be increased significantly to the medium-to-large range (targeting approximately 16-18pt, up from the previous 11-12pt) to ensure the three objectives plus their separators fill at least 65-70% of the container's vertical height, eliminating the severe under-fill (30-35% occupancy) from the previous attempt. This font size must match exactly the font size used in C0 to preserve within-slide uniformity (rule C6). The three numbered objective sections are distributed evenly across the full vertical height of the container using equal flexGrow values on each section, maximizing vertical spacing between items to confidently fill the available space. Thin dashed grey horizontal separator lines span the full column width between objectives (placed between objective 1 and 2, and between objective 2 and 3, but not after objective 3), creating visual rhythm and reinforcing the equal-weight structure of the three items. The container's left edge starts at approximately 70% of slide width (5% gutter from the left column's right edge); its right edge aligns to the slide's right safe-area margin. Top and bottom edges align exactly with the left column container (C0), creating a unified two-column row structure with strong horizontal alignment.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BOTTOM LAYER — Inactive): Not active. The slide is well-filled by the two content containers (C0 and C1), which confidently occupy the available vertical space from just below the title to near the slide bottom. With the font size increases applied to both columns, the content now fills 65-70% of each container's height, leaving purposeful whitespace rather than accidental gaps. No visual grouping or background fill is needed. The content containers sit directly on the white slide background, maintaining a clean, open aesthetic consistent with professional slide design principles.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER — Inactive): Not active. No cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements are needed. All content is self-contained within its respective column. The two-column layout is spatially independent — the Context section (C0) and Objectives section (C1) do not require visual linking elements.
